title: ls: cannot open directory '.': Permission denied in host mounted paths
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

If using a monted path we have a denied acess, this with root command too.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. set the envs for paths
2. run "podman run --rm -v $PACKAGES_DIR:/buildd -v $KERNEL_DIR:/buildd/sources -it quay.io/droidian/build-essential:trixie-amd64 bash"
3. if be to buildd we get a ls: cannot open directory '.': Permission denied


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

ls: cannot open directory '.': Permission denied

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

Enter in the Directory 

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.0.0-rc1
API Version:  5.0.0-rc1
Go Version:   go1.22.0
Built:        Sun Feb 11 20:38:54 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Running Fedora Silverblue Rawhide
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: it can be selinux, you either need to relabel the source (pass `:z` or `:Z` as a mount option), or temporarily disable it (`setenforce 0`).

> it can be selinux, you either need to relabel the source (pass `:z` or `:Z` as a mount option), or temporarily disable it (`setenforce 0`).

Oh thanks, i will try disable selinux.

My choice would be 

1. Try the :Z or :z, 
2. Disable separation for the container.
--security-opt label=disable
3. sudo setenforce 0

> @EliasTheGrandMasterOfMistakes any update?

Yes sorry for the delay, put SELINUX in permissive make all work great
